scope: Final internal milestone before external publish. Collapse duplicate TaintSource/TaintSink types in BOTH (a) tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs (struct duplicates of canonical taint::TaintSource/TaintSink) and (b) tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs (CLI-local const-pattern TaintSource/TaintSink + TaintTracker tree-sitter Python path). Route both the core 14-language substring scan_file_vulns AND the CLI Python tree-sitter analyze_python_file through canonical compute_taint_with_tree, mirroring the proven pattern at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:128. Add 4 ADDITIVE TaintSinkType variants (HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize) to bridge the canonical-engine ontology gap surfaced by Phase-1 investigation: canonical TaintSinkType has 6 variants (SqlQuery/CodeEval/CodeExec/CodeCompile/ShellExec/FileWrite) but tldr vuln emits 6 user-facing VulnTypes (4 of which — Xss, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization — have NO clean mapping today). Closes the tldr vuln FP class — the half of #24 that prior milestones (regex-removal-v1, field_access_info-extension-v1, sanitizer-removal-v1) all left open at the tldr vuln command path. analyze_rust_file (Rust line-scanner for UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic — pattern-detector concerns, not taint flow) is OUT OF SCOPE per Reframe C: compute_taint_with_tree is a CFG-based source-to-sink propagation engine, not a smell detector. JSON/SARIF output shape, CLI clap args, exit codes, and pub use exports preserved. After this lands + binary-verification confirms zero FPs across all fixtures, single coherent external cargo publish closes #7, #23, #24, #27, #28 + tldr vuln FP class + sanitizer correctness in one release.

validator_mandates.taintsinktype_extension_additive_only: M1 extends taint::TaintSinkType at taint.rs:153 with 4 NEW variants (HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize). MUST NOT rename or remove existing variants (SqlQuery, CodeEval, CodeExec, CodeCompile, ShellExec, FileWrite). M1 cargo check --workspace MUST surface every match site that becomes non-exhaustive — predicted: ~3 sites in tldr-cli/commands/taint.rs + bugbot/l2/ir.rs + tests. Each surfaced site MUST be extended with explicit arms (no wildcard).
validator_mandates.compute_taint_with_tree_dispatch_require: M3 (core vuln.rs scan_file_vulns rewrite) and M4 (CLI vuln.rs Python path collapse) MUST replace inline propagation with per-function compute_taint_with_tree calls. The proven pattern is at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:128 — duplicate this shape for the vuln command path. NO substring scanner residue allowed in the taint-flow path.
validator_mandates.m5_atomic: M5 deletion of ~36 obsolete unit tests + dead-code sweep MUST ship in ONE commit. Without atomicity, cargo test/clippy fail intermittently. M5 carries atomic_commit:true and release_commit_group:milestone_5_atomic. Mirrors sanitizer-removal-v1 M4 ATOMIC pattern.
validator_mandates.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: All test_e2e_* E2E tests at vuln.rs:1568-2100 (~30 tests covering parameterized queries, subprocess lists, type coercion, real SQLi, real CmdI, and 12 lang-specific command_injection + 13 lang-specific ssrf tests) MUST stay GREEN throughout M3 + M4 + M5. They are the primary user-facing behavior contract — any regression here is a release-blocker. Vuln type/severity/CWE mapping unit tests in remaining/vuln.rs (test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity) MUST stay GREEN.
validator_mandates.cli_output_shape_unchanged: tldr vuln --format json and --format sarif outputs MUST match HEAD-c842962 schema byte-for-byte modulo the taint_flow.path projection (Vec<usize> block IDs → Vec<String> human-readable, see plan §5.2). The CLI types::VulnFinding/VulnReport/VulnSummary structs in tldr-cli/commands/remaining/types.rs MUST NOT change shape. Existing vuln_sarif_deserialization_test.rs is the contract gate.
validator_mandates.rust_line_scanner_out_of_scope: analyze_rust_file at remaining/vuln.rs:747 (line-scanner for unsafe blocks, transmute, raw pointers, unwrap-in-non-test, mysql_query) MUST NOT be touched. It detects Rust-IDIOMATIC security smells (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic VulnTypes), NOT taint flows from sources to sinks. compute_taint_with_tree is the wrong tool for smell detection. Per Reframe C in plan §0.
validator_mandates.m1_test_enumeration_required: M1 MUST commit reports/M1-test-enumeration.json with ALL ~36 obsolete tests listed by file + line, including (a) ~5 bank-content unit tests in tldr-core/security/vuln.rs at L1322/1329/1335/1343/2077 (test_get_sources_python, test_get_sinks_sql_injection, test_get_sources_has_rust_and_php_coverage, test_get_sinks_has_csharp_and_elixir_coverage, test_get_sinks_ssrf_has_per_language_coverage); (b) ~17 propagation/sanitization helper tests at L1379-L1527 (test_extract_propagation, test_extract_assigned_variable_*, test_type_coercion_*, test_sanitized_sql_*, test_sanitized_command_*, test_unsanitized_*); (c) 4 CLI bank-tests in remaining/vuln.rs at L1844/1862/1900/test_string_interpolation_detection (test_is_taint_source, test_is_taint_sink, test_taint_tracker, test_string_interpolation_detection — verify M4 covers the f-string flow first); (d) ~5 vuln_type_name/builder helper tests for now-deleted scaffolding. M1 enumeration is GATING for M5: cargo test --workspace fails deterministically without it.
validator_mandates.performance_baseline_captured_m1: M1 captures wall-clock baseline on a canonical 20-file Go fixture set (path: continuum/autonomous/vuln-migration-v1-plan/perf-fixture/) by running `tldr vuln <fixture-dir> --lang go --format json > /dev/null` 3× and averaging. M3 stop-threshold gates on < 2× degradation. If exceeded: parallelize per-file with rayon (already a workspace dep) before declaring M3 GREEN.
validator_mandates.string_literal_fp_regression_mandatory: All ~66 string-literal regression-guard fixtures (2 fixtures × 33 X-marked language-vuln_type pairs in plan §4.2 matrix) MUST emit ZERO findings post-M3. NO carry-forward exceptions for the string-literal substring class. M5 stop-threshold runs the canonical /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go (--lang go) and fp2.ts (--lang typescript) and asserts ZERO findings — these are the original FP-repro fixtures from Phase-1 investigation.
validator_mandates.public_api_unchanged_external: pub use exports in tldr-core/src/security/mod.rs preserve same names. tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities signature preserved (only internals replaced). tldr vuln CLI clap args preserved. JSON/SARIF output schema preserved. Exit-code-2-on-findings behavior preserved. Per investigation §external_consumer_risk_assessment: LOW external-consumer risk.
validator_mandates.vuln_rs_internal_collapse_only: tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs may be reduced to a thin wrapper around per-function compute_taint_with_tree but MUST remain a module — DO NOT delete the file, DO NOT rename, DO NOT remove from mod.rs. After M3 the file should be ~400-500 LOC (down from 2162) containing: VulnType enum (RETAIN), VulnFinding/VulnSummary/VulnReport structs (RETAIN), scan_vulnerabilities public API (RETAIN as wrapper), get_remediation/get_cwe_id helpers (RETAIN), severity_for helper (NEW), the new scan_file_vulns body (NEW per-function compute_taint_with_tree loop), vuln_type_from_sink projection (NEW), descriptions_for(TaintSourceType, Language) lookup map (NEW — preserves descriptive 'Flask request.args (GET parameters)'-style strings for output compat). The retained E2E test suite at L1568-2100 stays in place (~30 tests).
validator_mandates.closes_issues_verified_binary: M5 stop-threshold runs the binary verification protocol: (1) cargo install --path crates/tldr-cli --features semantic --force; (2) tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go → ZERO findings; (3) tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/fp2.ts --lang typescript → ZERO findings; (4) tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.py --lang python → ZERO findings (negative-control regression — Python path was already FP-clean; verify still is post-M4 collapse onto canonical); (5) the full ~66 string-literal regression-guard fixture corpus → ZERO findings; (6) the full ~66 positive fixture corpus → ≥1 finding of correct VulnType each. Capture results to reports/M5-binary-verification.json. M5 commit BLOCKED until all 6 checks PASS.
validator_mandates.razor_java_constructor_carry_forward_doc: If M2 parity audit surfaces shape-fit gaps for Razor @Html.Raw(, Java new File(, or Ruby <%= raw( template-syntax that AST patterns + raw-fallback cannot address, document each in reports/M2-carry-forward.json with explicit rationale and amend the CHANGELOG ### Retained section in M6. Mirror the field_access_info-extension-v1 Ruby \bgets\b carry-forward pattern.
validator_mandates.no_taintinfo_taintflow_api_extension: TaintInfo / TaintFlow API at taint.rs:217-250 NOT extended. The 4 new TaintSinkType variants integrate via the existing TaintSink.sink_type field (which is already TaintSinkType-typed). NO new fields on TaintInfo, TaintFlow, TaintSource, or TaintSink in this milestone.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!vuln-migration-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's files + the source code edits per milestone are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged (`git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty).

ADD variant `HttpRequest` with doc-comment 'Outbound HTTP/URL request (SSRF sink)'
ADD variant `Deserialize` with doc-comment 'Untrusted-data deserialization (RCE-via-deser sink)'
DO NOT yet wire AST detection for these variants — M2 work.

milestones.rollback_rule: If any post-commit assertion fails, REVERT entire M5 commit and re-investigate. NO partial-fix follow-up commits — preserves atomic boundary.

milestones.title: CHANGELOG entry + local tag

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per regex-removal-v1 W3-M12 / field_access_info-extension-v1 M6 / sanitizer-removal-v1 M5 precedent
sections: ### Changed: tldr vuln command now routes through canonical compute_taint_with_tree for all 16 supported languages (was: per-language substring scanner in tldr-core/security/vuln.rs for 14 languages + CLI-local tree-sitter TaintTracker for Python). Eliminates the string-literal substring FP class (closes #24's tldr vuln half — the half left open by regex-removal-v1, field_access_info-extension-v1, and sanitizer-removal-v1, all of which only reached the tldr taint command path).

### Added: TaintSinkType variants HtmlOutput (XSS), FileOpen (path-traversal, distinct from existing FileWrite), HttpRequest (SSRF), Deserialize. AST sink-bank entries (~218) for the 4 new variants across all 16 LanguagePatterns.

### Removed: tldr-core/security/vuln.rs duplicate TaintSource/TaintSink structs (collapsed onto canonical). tldr-cli/commands/remaining/vuln.rs CLI-local TaintSource/TaintSink const tables + TaintTracker + analyze_python_file (~700 LOC). scan_file_vulns substring scanner + 8 inline-propagation helpers + per-language source/sink Vec tables in core vuln.rs (~500 LOC). ~26 obsolete unit tests across both files.

### Retained: VulnType enum + VulnFinding/VulnSummary/VulnReport output records. scan_vulnerabilities public API (now thin wrapper). get_remediation/get_cwe_id helpers. analyze_rust_file Rust line-scanner (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic — distinct concern from taint flow). All ~30 test_e2e_* tests + all CLI integration tests. JSON/SARIF output shape. Exit-code-2-on-findings behavior.

### Architectural note: This is the FINAL internal milestone before external publish. Together with regex-removal-v1, field_access_info-extension-v1, and sanitizer-removal-v1, the canonical tldr-core/security/taint.rs is now the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for taint flow detection across both tldr taint and tldr vuln. The string-literal substring FP class (closes-#24-shaped) is eliminated end-to-end. After this milestone lands + binary verification confirms zero FPs, a single coherent external cargo publish closes #7, #23, #24, #27, #28 + tldr vuln FP class + sanitizer correctness in one release.

green_files.purpose: Final summary: LOC delta, FP-closure verdict, TaintSinkType extensions, performance regression results, binary-verification outputs.

stop_thresholds: CHANGELOG entry merged.
Local annotated tag vuln-migration-v1 applied.
NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.
reports/M6-release-prep.md committed.

per_language_verdict.Python: GO — already FP-clean via tree-sitter analyze_python_file at HEAD; M4 collapses onto canonical AST path (parity verified via E2E tests at vuln.rs:1568-1672 + M1 f-string regression).
per_language_verdict.TypeScript_JavaScript: GO — currently FP-prone via core scan_vulnerabilities; M3 collapse routes through canonical compute_taint_with_tree. FP repro at HEAD verified.
per_language_verdict.Go: GO — currently FP-prone; FP repro at HEAD verified (3 false positives on string_literal_fp.go).
per_language_verdict.Java: GO — currently FP-prone; M2 may need raw-fallback for `new File(` constructor shape (carry-forward candidate per plan §9).
per_language_verdict.Rust: OUT OF SCOPE for taint-flow migration (Reframe C). analyze_rust_file line-scanner retained for UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic. Note: taint-flow VulnTypes (SqlInjection/Xss/etc.) for Rust ARE in scope and route through canonical via the M3 rewrite.
per_language_verdict.C: GO — currently FP-prone via core scan_vulnerabilities.
per_language_verdict.Ruby: GO with M2 audit — Rack::Utils.escape_html scope_resolution shape and `<%= raw(` template-syntax shape may need raw-fallback (carry-forward candidate per plan §9).
per_language_verdict.CSharp: GO with M2 audit — Razor `@Html.Raw(` operator-prefix shape may need raw-fallback (carry-forward candidate per plan §9).
per_language_verdict.Lua_Luau: GO — currently FP-prone (small bank, low complexity).

vuln_rs_get_sources_get_sinks_per_langua.reason: After M3 these per-language Vec tables become DELETED (substring scanner replaced by canonical AST sink banks). NOT retained.

scan_vulnerabilities_public_api.reason: Public API signature preserved. Internals are fully replaced with per-function compute_taint_with_tree loop. Preserves backward compatibility for any external library consumer.

vuln_type_vulnfinding_output_records.reason: User-facing ontology + JSON/SARIF output schema. Preserved exactly.

analyze_rust_file.reason: Rust line-scanner for UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic — distinct concern from taint flow. Out of scope per Reframe C.
analyze_rust_file.deferred_to_milestone: TBD followon (rust-smell-detector-canonical-v1) if a canonical smell-detector is built. Not part of vuln-migration-v1.

carry_forward_exceptions.reason: If M2 parity audit surfaces a gap that no AST or raw-fallback shape can address, retain as carry-forward (analogous to field_access_info-extension-v1 Ruby \bgets\b).
carry_forward_exceptions.preliminary_status: ANTICIPATED candidates: Razor @Html.Raw( (CSharp), Java new File( (constructor), Ruby <%= raw( (template). M2 audit verifies.

loc_delta_summary.note: Net deletion of ~685 LOC. The ~280 LOC M1 RED tests + ~400 LOC M2 AST bank entries are NEW additions; balanced against ~1400 LOC of deletion across M3 + M4 + M5. Numbers approximate; executor sees true delta in git diff.

investigation_findings.critical_reframe_a: Duplicate-types span TWO files (not one). tldr-core/security/vuln.rs has TaintSource/TaintSink at L68/L81 (struct duplicates of canonical taint.rs types with semantically-divergent fields). tldr-cli/commands/remaining/vuln.rs has its OWN TaintSource/TaintSink at L114/~210 (DIFFERENT shape — static const-pattern tables for the Python tree-sitter analyze_python_file path) plus a CLI-local TaintTracker at L335 (~700 LOC of intra-procedural propagation). Migration requires TWO independent collapses, not one.
investigation_findings.critical_reframe_b: Canonical TaintSinkType at taint.rs:153 has 6 variants but tldr vuln emits 6 user-facing VulnTypes 4 of which (Xss, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization) have NO clean TaintSinkType mapping. Migration requires +4 ADDITIVE enum variants (HtmlOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize) plus AST sink-bank entries across all 16 LanguagePatterns. This is broader than the handoff implied but stays within ADDITIVE-public-API-extension boundaries (existing variants unchanged).
investigation_findings.critical_reframe_c: analyze_rust_file at remaining/vuln.rs:747 (line-scanner for UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic) is OUT OF SCOPE. compute_taint_with_tree is a CFG-based source-to-sink propagation engine, not a smell detector. analyze_rust_file's concerns (unsafe blocks, transmute, raw pointers, unwrap-in-non-test) are pattern-detection smells, not taint flows. Retained as-is.
investigation_findings.fp_repro_verified_at_head: tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go --lang go → 3 FALSE POSITIVE CommandInjection findings, all citing string-literal source lines. tldr vuln /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/fp2.ts --lang typescript → 1 FALSE POSITIVE CommandInjection citing a COMMENT line as the sink. Python negative-control: tldr vuln string_literal_fp.py → 0 findings (Python tree-sitter path is FP-clean for string-literal class).
investigation_findings.compute_taint_with_tree_signature: pub fn compute_taint_with_tree(cfg: &CfgInfo, refs: &[VarRef], statements: &HashMap<u32, String>, tree: Option<&tree_sitter::Tree>, source: Option<&[u8]>, language: Language, ssa: Option<&SsaFunction>) -> Result<TaintInfo, TldrError> at taint.rs:3676. Existing in-tree callers: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:128 (the proven precedent for tldr taint), bugbot/l2/ir.rs:105, l2_api_validation_test.rs:719, plus ~10 integration test files in tldr-core/tests/. Adding tldr vuln as the 5th in-tree caller mirrors the proven pattern.
investigation_findings.test_coverage_at_head: Core vuln.rs has ~50 tests at L1322-L2100: ~5 bank-content (DELETE in M5), ~17 propagation/sanitization helpers (DELETE in M5), ~30 test_e2e_* (PRESERVE — primary regression guard). CLI remaining/vuln.rs has ~10 tests: ~4 bank/tracker (DELETE in M5), 6 test_analyze_rust_* (PRESERVE — Rust line-scanner regression). CLI integration tests (vuln_autodetect_tests, vuln_ssrf_test, vuln_sarif_deserialization_test) all PRESERVED.
investigation_findings.taintsinktype_extension_match_sites: M1 cargo check --workspace expected to surface ~3 match-on-TaintSinkType sites needing extension: tldr-cli/commands/taint.rs:326 (TaintSinkType import + format_taint_text match), bugbot/l2/ir.rs (similar usage), test files. Each surfaced site MUST be extended with explicit arms (no wildcards permitted per validator_mandates.taintsinktype_extension_additive_only).
investigation_findings.performance_consideration: Per-function CFG/DFG/SSA construction is heavier than substring scanning. M1 captures wall-clock baseline on 20-file Go fixture; M3 stop-threshold gates on < 2× degradation. If exceeded, parallelize per-file with rayon (already a workspace dep). Mitigation per-risk R3 + R8.
investigation_findings.predecessor_milestones: regex-removal-v1 (eliminated regex sources/sinks for tldr taint, 13 langs), field_access_info-extension-v1 (eliminated regex sources/sinks for Ruby/Elixir/OCaml), sanitizer-removal-v1 (eliminated regex sanitizers across 16 langs). All three only reached tldr taint command path. vuln-migration-v1 closes the parallel substring scanner + CLI-local TaintTracker for tldr vuln, completing the canonical-pipeline coverage.

premortem_risks.R1_taintsinktype_extension_breaks_extern: HIGH if not careful, LOW with mitigation. Mandate: additive variants only; cargo check --workspace surfaces all match sites in M1 for explicit arm extension.
premortem_risks.R2_compute_taint_with_tree_metadata_insu: MEDIUM. TaintFlow includes source, sink, path. M3 verifies projected output matches existing test_e2e_* expectations.
premortem_risks.R3_performance_regression_per_function_c: MEDIUM. M1 captures baseline; M3 stop-threshold gates on < 2× degradation; rayon fallback available.
premortem_risks.R4_tree_parsing_unavailable_for_some_flo: LOW. tldr vuln has no --from-stdin variant; all input is file path. Fallback to non-tree mode preserved at taint.rs:3753.
premortem_risks.R5_razor_java_constructor_ruby_template_: MEDIUM. M2 parity audit verifies; carry-forward exception pattern available (sanitizer-removal-v1 retained Ruby \bgets\b precedent).
premortem_risks.R6_external_lib_callers_depend_on_descri: MEDIUM. M3 preserves descriptive 'Flask request.args (GET parameters)'-style via descriptions_for(TaintSourceType, Language) helper retaining the existing description map even after the source-pattern Vec is deleted.
premortem_risks.R7_python_fstring_interpolation_canonica: LOW-MEDIUM. M1 RED test for f-string flow; M4 verifies parity. If gap surfaces, extend canonical extract_interpolated_vars (same shape that handled it for tldr taint).
premortem_risks.R8_perf_regression_specifically_many_sma: MEDIUM. M1 baseline catches; if surfaces in M3, batch CFG construction across functions in same file (single tree-sitter walk → multi-CFG output).
premortem_risks.R9_analyze_rust_file_overlap_with_taint_: LOW. analyze_rust_file emits UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic (distinct from SqlInjection/Xss/etc.). Audit confirms zero overlap at HEAD.
premortem_risks.R10_premature_m5_atomic_commit_before_m3: HIGH if not careful. Sequencing enforced by depends:[M4] + atomic_commit:true on M5. Worker-loop verifies M4 GREEN before staging M5.

recommended_pre_autonomous_steps: Run discriminative premortem (pattern caught real blockers in 2 of 2 prior milestones).
Capture wall-clock baseline at HEAD via canonical 20-file Go fixture.
Verify external publish gate alignment with maintainer (issues #7, #23, #24, #27, #28 + tldr vuln FP class + sanitizer correctness).
